train of the Northern Pacific at
Sprague was narrowly averted. The
incident happened as the result of
the men having to load baggage. The
passenger train had been running
three hours later and when leaving
and 20 minutes behind time. After
starting out again the passenger train
met a westbound freight and was
stepped just in time to prevent an
accident.


1t was also reported at the teleg-
raphers' headquarters that a freight
train had been stalled at Cocollalla
for 81 hours and it became necessary
to kill the engine. Pickets have
reported but three operators working
between Spokane and Trout Creek.


l.. a. Tanquary, deputy president
of the Order of Railway Telegraph
ers, received telegram from Ellens-
burg this morning which stated that
the three men arrested last night had
been released. Mr.. Tanquary also
said the report of the operators tam
pering with the trains was all tom
myrot. "'The operators have worked
with the engineers and trainmen for
years." he said, land they are mutual
friends. No operator would lift his
hand to jeopardize the lives of any
of the trainmen."
